Ref

```
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Read(//mnt/c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)",
      "Read(//c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)",
      "Bash(cd:*)",
      "Bash(python3:*)",
      "Bash(cmake ..:*)",
      "Bash(cmake:*)",
      "Bash(sed -i 's/\\(uint8_t\\)clamp\\(r, 0.0f, 255.0f\\)/\\(uint8_t\\)fminf\\(fmaxf\\(r, 0.0f\\), 255.0f\\)/g' src/cuda_kernels.cu)",
      "Bash(sed -n *p *)",
      "Bash(sed -i *[0-9,]*[ad] *)",
      "Bash(sed -i *r /tmp/* *)",
      "Bash(sed -i '.*' src/*.cpp)",
      "Bash(grep:*)",
      "Bash(head:*)",
      "Bash(tail:*)",
      "Bash(cat:*)",
      "Bash(echo:*)",
      "Bash(mkdir -p src/**)",
      "Bash(mv src/* src/**)",
      "Edit(//mnt/c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)",
      "Edit(//c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)",
      "Write(//mnt/c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)",
      "Write(//c/[PROJECT PATH]/**)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Bash(rm:*)",
      "Bash(sudo:*)",
      "Bash(chmod *777*)",
      "Bash(chown:*)",
      "Bash(dd:*)",
      "Bash(mkfs:*)",
      "Bash(eval:*)",
      "Bash(curl:*)",
      "Bash(wget:*)",
      "Bash(* | sh*)",
      "Bash(* ; *)",
      "Bash(* &)",
      "Bash(* /etc/*)",
      "Bash(* /usr/*)",
      "Bash(* /var/*)",
      "Bash(* ~/.ssh/*)",
      "Bash(* ..*)",
      "Bash(* //*)",
      "Bash(* C:\\Windows\\*)",
      "Bash(* Program Files*)"
    ],
    "ask": [
      "Bash(git:*)",
      "Bash(git * *)"
    ]
  }
}

```

_**Agent (claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking)**_

Here's a summary of what changed:

**`settings.json` — shared (committed to repo)**

- **Allow added:** `node_modules/.bin/*`, `yarn`, `npm`, `npx`, `pnpm` — covers linting, tests, and installs without prompting
- **Ask added:** `git branch -d`, `git branch -D`, `git worktree remove` — will prompt before deleting branches or worktrees

**`settings.local.json` — machine-local (not committed)**

- Removed the now-redundant `yarn test`, `npx vitest`, `node_modules/.bin/vitest`, and `yarn install` entries since they're covered by the broader `yarn:*` and `node_modules/.bin/*` rules in shared settings

The shared `settings.json` now has a full set of allowed git read/write commands:

_**Agent (claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking)**_

The issue is that `cd` is not in the allow list in `~/.claude/settings.json`, so the compound command `cd ... && git log ...` is blocked because it starts with `cd`.

_**Agent (claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking)**_

The `Bash` permission matcher checks the **beginning** of the full command string. Since `cd /path && git log ...` starts with `cd`, none of the existing `git *` rules matched it — it fell through to ask/deny.

Adding `Bash(cd:*)` to `allow` fixes it. The `&&`-chained commands after the `cd` are already covered by the other rules.
